description_long: This shotgun features a robust, angular design with a dark, mottled metal body and distinct wooden elements at the stock and fore-end. The dual-barrel configuration is evident, with both barrels running parallel and capped with metallic muzzle ends. The stock and grip are constructed from a reddish-brown wood, providing a stark contrast to the otherwise industrial appearance. A rectangular trigger guard and a visible trigger are present, with a small metallic lever or latch above the trigger area. The top of the receiver is flat and unadorned, while the overall form is blocky and utilitarian, suggesting a focus on function over aesthetics. The shotgun's finish is matte, with visible wear and texture on the metal surfaces.
description: A double-barreled shotgun with a dark, textured metal body and contrasting wooden stock and fore-end, featuring a blocky, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the shotgun, highlighting the dual barrels, wooden stock, and metallic trigger guard.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the twin barrel openings and the squared-off wooden fore-end.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the dual barrels and wooden stock, with visible details of the receiver and trigger area.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the wooden stock and the back of the receiver, with the barrels extending away from the viewer.
attribute_barrel_configuration: side-by-side double-barrel, both barrels of equal length and no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rectangular, medium-sized, with a straight profile and no visible finger grooves or reinforcement
